title: archangelica archangelica
intro: Archangelica archangelica, also known as angelica or wild celery, is a biennial plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. The roots, stems, and leaves of this plant have been used in herbal medicine for centuries, due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of archangelica archangelica include its ability to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and ease menstrual cramps, while also providing antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Archangelica archangelica can be prepared in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used in cooking and as a natural remedy for various health issues.
